items.title: Health Minister denies health cuts, despite asking department to find $700m in cuts

children.content: Health groups fear the impact of cuts expected by the Health Department as part of a state budget operational efficiencies program.

gallery.altText: A female doctor carrying a stethoscope.

thumbnail.altText: A female doctor carrying a stethoscope.

items.title: When the bushfire came, this couple turned on their roof sprinkler and left

children.content: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.

image.altText: Aerial perspective of a coastal home spinkler system on roof surrounded by burned trees under a blue sky.

items.title: 'How are you, mate?' Bodycam footage shown of police officer's fatal shooting

children.content: A court hears that some of the last words uttered by a policeman was to ask the man who fatally shot him, "how are you, mate?"

image.altText: Keith Smith and Leigh Sushames composite image.

items.title: These aren't ants but shipping containers escaping on a massive breakaway iceberg

children.content: Details of how the shipping containers ended up on a giant iceberg have been revealed in one of the more unusual reports tabled at the annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations.

articles.title: China and Russia block conservation bid for endangered emperor penguin

children.content: The annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations has failed to reach consensus to designate the endangered emperor penguin as a "Specially Protected Species".

image.altText: Several adult penguins and over a dozen chicks stand in a snowy landscape.

gallery.altText: A large rectangular iceberg with seven dark dots on it, seen from a satellite photo.

thumbnail.altText: A large rectangular iceberg with seven dark dots on it, seen from a satellite photo.

items.title: Dismay over 150-year-old house being reduced to rubble

children.content: How did a colonial-era mansion once earmarked to be on the state heritage register get bulldozed?

image.altText: An old black and white photograph of a house at 180 George Street, Launceston.

items.title: Rex Airlines announces service cuts in Victoria and Tasmania

children.content: The embattled carrier has announced it is ceasing some services and reducing others.

image.altText: a plane flies in the sky

items.title: Sweeping cuts but little detail — can the government stick to the Abetz budget?

children.content: Since Eric Abetz became Tasmania's treasurer nine months ago, there have been two prevailing questions. How much will be cut? And what will be cut? As of yesterday, we know how much, but don't know what or where.

articles.title: What's being cut in Tasmania's state budget?

children.content: Treasurer Eric Abetz wants to find $1.47 billion in "efficiencies" in Tasmania's state budget. This is what's on the chopping block.

image.altText: Tas budget cuts cards graphic showing six categories.

articles.title: 'Hard to achieve, in our opinion': Ratings agency unimpressed with Tas budget

children.content: Credit rating agency S&P Global says Tasmania's budget repair ambitions face "high execution risk". The government aims to save $1.5 billion through cuts.

image.altText: A photo taken over the shoulder of a male holding the blue Tasmanian Budget book.

gallery.altText: Tasmanian Treasurer Eric Abetz and Premier Jeremy Rockliff in the budget lock-up for the 2026-27 state budget.

thumbnail.altText: Tasmanian Treasurer Eric Abetz and Premier Jeremy Rockliff in the budget lock-up for the 2026-27 state budget.

items.title: How the Musk v Altman case revealed an 'uncomfortable' truth

children.content: After Sam Altman and OpenAI's recent victory in a drawn-out legal battle with billionaire Elon Musk, the bitter rivalry between the former friends is now set to move from the courtroom to Wall Street.

gallery.altText: Elon Musk sits on chair on stage next to Sam Altman and an interviewer.

thumbnail.altText: Elon Musk sits on chair on stage next to Sam Altman and an interviewer.

items.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's director of national ​intelligence

children.content: Tulsi Gabbard says she is departing from the role to support her husband after his recent bone cancer diagnosis.

image.altText: A woman wearing white speaks into a microphone as she looks diagonally to her right

items.title: For Willie Rioli now is time to 'give back' to the place that gave him everything

children.content: The Tiwi Islands are unique and Australian Rules football is a way of life that has spawned some of the game's biggest names. But even as favourite son Willie Rioli Jr returns home, the Tiwi Bombers know they need more help.

image.altText: A man sits on the grass shirtless next to other footy players. Some other men are standing in the background.

items.title: The mission to save conjoined twin involved an airlift, five surgeons and a heartbreaking sacrifice

children.content: A team of doctors involved in the separation of conjoined Papua New Guinean twins Sawong and Tom have spoken for the first time about the rare and complex case.

gallery.altText: A close up of a Papua New Guinean baby smiling from a cot.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a Papua New Guinean baby smiling from a cot.
Conjoined twins lie together on a small cot.

items.title: ASIO holiday warning did not flag elevated Hanukkah threat

children.content: Australia's intelligence agency, ASIO, issued its annual holiday threat assessment less than two weeks before the Hanukkah terrorist attack at Bondi Beach but did not identify heightened risks to the Jewish festival, despite earlier declaring antisemitism as its top priority in terms of threat to life.

image.altText: People hug at floral tribute Bondi Beach promenade

items.title: Children's entertainer Danny Go!'s son dies of cancer aged 14

children.content: The popular children's YouTuber revealed the news after documenting the illness since it was diagnosed late last year. Tributes have flowed from Children's entertainers, including the Wiggles.

image.altText: The YouTuber shared the news of his son's diagnosis in December, last year.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX launches upgraded Starship

children.content: Elon Musk's rocket company has launched its new Starship design to enable more frequent Starlink satellite launches and to send future NASA missions to the moon.

image.altText: People looking over the ocean watching a rocket take off

items.title: Police investigate triple fatal crash in Sydney's south

children.content: Three people have died in a car crash in Sydney's south, with police attempting to conduct a welfare check at one of the victim's homes minutes before the incident

image.altText: Police and fire cars parked near a crashed car.

items.title: Suzie Miller took the risky path away from a safe job as a judge, with no regrets

children.content: In 2009, Suzie Miller had a choice between becoming a magistrate in Australia or a residency at the National Theatre in London.

items.title: The prickly plant powering a new spirits industry

children.content: Australia's emerging agave industry is attracting interest from distillers, farmers and scientists who believe the spiky plant could transform everything from tequila production to sustainable aviation fuel.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of Prince, Twin Peaks and some very important donkeys

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

children.content: Crispy artichokes and textural grain make this salad a satisfying dinner and excellent next-day lunch.

items.title: Is Australia's two-party political system dead?

children.content: With the rise of One Nation's popularity and younger voters increasingly turning to the Greens, the traditional two-party system in Australian politics has been dismantled, a former political strategist argues.

image.altText: Composite of Greens volunteers holding placards and Pauline Hanson meeting One Nation voters

items.title: Female veterans find solace in surf with 'life-changing' therapy

children.content: A Queensland woman has launched a program designed specifically for women veterans to improve their mental health.

items.title: Call to 911 reveals harrowing moments before NASCAR champ's death

children.content: A call to 911 reveals NASCAR champion Kyle Busch had been coughing up blood in the hours before his tragic death aged 41.

items.title: Psychiatrist repeatedly harassed nurse on messaging platforms

children.content: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a doctor noting down symptoms of a patient on a form with a stethoscope hanging around his neck.

items.title: Black mould and broken toilets: Inside 'dismal' government-owned homes

children.content: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.

items.title: WA health official wants more support to stem 'rare' diphtheria outbreak

children.content: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

image.altText: A nurse preparing a boostrix vaccination, the vaccine's white and purple box is visible in the foreground.

items.title: Collingwood champion's achievement reopens old wound outside Victoria

children.content: When Scott Pendlebury steps onto the MCG on Saturday afternoon, his name will stand alone at the top of a remarkable list — but the achievement has also reignited a debate about the value of non-Victorian Aussie Rules records.

image.altText: Craig Bradley moves the ball forward for South Australia.

items.title: Tony Albert's form of optimism is a lesson for us all

items.title: WHO upgrades risk assessment for Ebola outbreak in DRC to 'very high'

items.title: Top End tourism and hospitality businesses seek lifeline amid rising costs

items.title: Plastic waste collected in Sydney Harbour during Vivid festival rising

children.content: Data from floating rubbish bins near the Sydney CBD suggests there are higher levels of rubbish entering the water when major events such as Vivid are on.
